name: resolve_4k_ignores_2m_ps
code:
proof fn phi_4_resolve_4k_ignores_2m_ps(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index, l2i: L2Index, l1i: L1Index)
    requires
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        0 <= l2i < 512,
        0 <= l1i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i).is_Some(),
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i).unwrap().perm.ps == true,
        pt.l1_tables@.dom().contains(pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i).unwrap().addr),
        pt.l1_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l2(l4i, l3i, l2i).unwrap().addr].value()[l1i].perm.present,
    ensures
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_4k_l1(l4i, l3i, l2i, l1i).is_Some(),
{
}
reason: spec_resolve_mapping_4k_l1 does not check ps on the L2 entry — it will resolve 4k pages through an L2 entry that has ps=true (marking it as a 2MB page), creating conflicting 4k and 2m interpretations of the same address range
